Recap: Training, Validation, and Test Sets
D
A training set is what we use to train our model (e.g ., find the 00, 0 ,, ... values in OLS)
Instructor
A validation set is a "held-out" portion of the training set that can be used more than once.
A test set is what we use to evaluate our model once, at the very end.
Train model on training set. Evaluate model on the validation set. Adjust model. Repeat.
At the very end, evaluate on the test set once.

For a dataset with K folds:
Instructor
Pick one fold to hold out.
Train model on data from every other fold.
Compute the error on the held-out fold.
Repeat for all K folds.
The cross-validation (CV) error is the average error across all K held-out folds.

Cross-Validation
The validation set gave us an opportunity to understand how the model performs on a single set of unseen data. The specific validation set we drew was fixed - we
used the same validation points every time.
It's possible that we may have, by random chance, selected a set of validation points that was not representative of other unseen data that the model might encounter
(for example, if we happened to have selected all outlying data points for the validation set).
Different train/validation splits lead to different validation errors:

To apply cross-validation, we use the KFold class of sklearn. model_selection . KFold will return the indices of each cross-validation fold. Then, we iterate
over each of these folds to designate it as the validation set, while training the model on the remaining folds.

[Terminology] Probability and Random Variables (RVs)
The outcomes of RVs have corresponding probabilities.
Instructor
Discrete RV: Each possible outcome has an associated probability.
For coin flips, P(X=1) = 0.5 and P(X=0) = 0.5
Continuous RV: Individual outcomes have probability 0. No single event is "special"!
For example, if Y represents airtime of coin flips, P(Y = 2.712324 seconds) = 0
But, ranges of outcomes have probability ≥ 0.
For example, we could have a situation where P(0 seconds < Y < 2 seconds) = 0.8
